pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh lines 51-79 where file is created dynamically
Referenced in task.json metadata and removed/created by setup_task.sh line 83
Microsoft Learn DefaultNotificationsSetting and DefaultGeolocationSetting documentation confirm: AllowNotifications (1), AllowGeolocation (1)
Microsoft Learn DefaultNotificationsSetting and DefaultGeolocationSetting documentation confirm: BlockNotifications (2), BlockGeolocation (2)
youtube.com is a real domain; notifications is a valid Edge/Chrome permission type; setting 2 (Block) is verified
facebook.com is a real domain; notifications is a valid Edge/Chrome permission type; setting 2 (Block) is verified
teams.microsoft.com is a real domain; notifications is a valid Edge/Chrome permission type; setting 1 (Allow) is verified
zoom.us is a real domain; media_stream_camera is confirmed as valid technical parameter in Edge/Chrome Preferences; setting 1 (Allow) is verified
maps.google.com is a real domain; geolocation is confirmed in Microsoft Learn as valid Edge permission type; setting 1 (Allow) is verified

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Microsoft Edge site-specific permissions for five domains in a lab environment. Setting values use standard Chromium/Edge numeric encoding: 1 = Allow, 2 = Block (verified against Microsoft Learn policy documentation). Permission types are: notifications (blocked for youtube.com and facebook.com to prevent distraction, allowed for teams.microsoft.com for remote lectures), media_stream_camera (allowed for zoom.us for virtual sessions), and geolocation (allowed for maps.google.com for Geography 101). All domains are real, publicly-accessible websites. The requirements and report files are created dynamically by setup scripts at task runtime, making the data synthetic rather than from external sources.
